He's our apartment IQ product expert.
So he's gonna go through the platform.
Um But we actually met with Gabrielle a few weeks ago to discuss Apartment IQ.
If you're not familiar with apartment IQ, it's a B I tool and essentially we automate the market survey process com data collection process for you.
It's completely an automated tool and it's updated every single day.
It's updated every 24 hours.
And what's fantastic about it is we're actually able to track any competitor as long as they have a website and use online leasing.
Um It's all public data, so it's all coming from their property websites.
Um And Gabriela thought um you all would have some interest.
She had quite a bit of interest.
Um So if you have any questions to start off, feel free to ask questions.
But um Justin's gonna go through the platform and it's definitely supposed to be interactive.
So if you have anything, just while he's going through it, feel free to um jump in.
Cool, nice to meet everybody.
Appreciate you jumping on.
Um I guess before I get go, get started any kind of quick questions here, I don't know how much you know about it other than kind of what Jamie just mentioned.
Um I know looking back at my notes, one thing that I remember is that you guys are performing the, the monthly market surveys right now and, and kind of inputting that data into a spreadsheet.
Um I do want you to know that in terms of the the data we provide, uh we do have a like an in app experience here which you're looking at a dashboard.
Um If you prefer to work out of Excel, all of our data is downloadable, exportable to CS P or Excel.
Um so we can get you the data however you do want it.
Um So whatever is, is is preferred, you know, we can definitely take that route.
Um But yeah, so I mean, looking at the screen here, this would be your apartment IQ landing page.
Uh Each one of your properties would actually get their own individual property card and then we would run the database off of that property specific comp set.
Uh What is great is that your team is always in complete control of your comp set.
So typically, what happens whenever we set these up is that you would provide a list of comps that you've been tracking and we would build the data that way.
Um You also would have access to our comp matching algorithm.
So essentially what we do is we look at uh properties in the vicinity of your property and we let you know how well how well they comp out.
Uh We take into account about five different factors.
Um Those factors include proximity.
We also look at your built or renovated, we look at asset class, we'll look at price and then we'll even do some um amenity matching and then we'll, we'll make those recommendations.

So, I think that's a nice feature in, in some of those markets that you might not be particularly familiar with or if there's a lot of new inventory coming on board or a lot of renovations going on, um, just making sure that you're tracking everybody that, that is relevant to you.
Um, what else is nice is that you can always edit comp sets directly inside the app itself as well.
So if you do ever wanna add a property or remove a property, you can do it yourself, you know, right here in the app.
Um The last thing that I will mention is that we also offer um unlimited users.
So we don't charge per seat.
So I think you'll see here in a second, but this is something that does offer value from the on site team all the way up.
So whoever you would want to give access to the platform, um we can set them up.
Uh We also provide tons of training.
So group sessions, one on one sessions, ongoing training sessions.
Um We do a really nice job of making sure that every user is able to kind of, you know, see the value in the product and, and and access the data that is relevant to them.
But yeah, nonetheless.
So like I said, each property gets its own card and then to view the analytics for that property, you click the view button and it'll take you inside the data.
Uh Once you're in here, then the very top, this is your main navigation.
So each property will have four reports associated with it.
The first report, which we're on is the market survey.
And then if you select the data drop down, you'll see three additional reports as well.
So I'll go over each one very high level for you.
Um Before I do though, I want you to know that all of the data inside of apartment IQ is filterable by these different options.
So you can look at the data based on a specific comp or even a subset of comps.
Uh You can also look at a be based off of bedrooms, bathrooms, square footage range, rent, price ranges, et cetera.
And then down below here, all of this data is sort from low to high and high to low.
But starting with the market survey here, I think very simple, very straightforward, very representative of your state market survey.
I think we've done a really nice job of making it extremely easy to compare your property's performance versus the competition.
So inside of this report, you'll notice two different sections.
Um The first one being property summary, that's all of your high level property data, a lot of performance analytics.
And then the second section down here is pricing and availability broken up by floor plan.
But nonetheless, what we do here is we list every one of your competitors across the top.
And then we also include your property or the subject property, which is highlighted in the indigo.
Again, that way you can compare your property's performance versus the competition.
So some of the things we do provide here in the property, uh summary section would be, you know, distance of proximity for each comp from your property.
We also look at your built total number of units we do present um advertised occupancy percentage.
So I'll come back to this in a second.
Uh We also show you things like exposure percentage.
So the percent of units that are available inside of each property, um You can see that we present that over three different time horizons.
So total exposure exposure of the next 30 next 60 days, we will show you the number of units that are available inside of each property of those units that are available, how many are vacant?
Then we'll also provide some transactional data.
So the number of leases that each property has had over the last 30 days, over the last seven days and the number of leases that each property averages per week.
And lastly, what's great is we do track rent, specials and concessions as well.
So we'll let you know if they're tied to the property or specific floor plan or a unit inside of that property.

And lastly, in terms of this section, um what's great is we take the concessions and we'll actually calculate net effective rent and that effective price per foot for you at the property level at the floor plan level and all the way down to the actual unit level itself.
So any questions here?
So with your comps and Justin, I know you shared this with me, right?
Um Your product is different from Costar, right?

And where apartment IQ is different from Costar is you're taking information on active properties and your algorithm is really and you have people that are checking the websites for each of the properties individually where Costar is pulling from I OS feeds to marketing companies.
And Costar also pulls in new construction where apartment IQ is not necessarily pulling new construction if it's not on the website.
So there's a bunch of information that Costar provides that we're not going to find here here.
It's more apartments rents and availability, right?
And how you're tracking that.
Yeah, that, that's absolutely correct.
Yeah.
So our tool goes out to those property websites.
We collect the data every single night.
So everything we're presenting here is exactly what a renter would see whenever you know he or she visits your website, visit the competitors website.
Um in terms of the quality of the data too.
So, I mean, Apartments or apartments.com co star is gonna have great data on those apartments.com customers.
Um Only about 50% of properties out there are on apartments.com right now.
Um The way they actually collect the data for the other 50% is they have call farms set up where they actually make phone calls into subsets of, of, of markets every single month.
The issue with that is they don't actually touch each property and again, they only touch it once a month.
So if that person doesn't pick up the phone that month, that data is not updated until the next month.
So there is an issue there with um with coverage and also too, as far as the data that's on apartments.com, there's typically limitations in terms of uh of the uh uh in terms of availability.
So if they have a couple of different styles of one bedrooms available, you know, typically apartments.com will cap it at five units for each floor plan.
Um Whereas with apartment IQ, I mean, we can get everything that is made available on, on the property website.
So oftentimes our availabilities, you know, a lot more depth into our availability, which is going to impact a lot of performance metrics as well.

Um And yes, to your point about new construction, we don't necessarily report um what's in the pipeline, but as soon as a new construction does go live with pre leasing, it's automatically added to our database and we can start reporting the, the rents and, and and the performance metrics from there.
So that's a, that's absolutely correct.

you mentioned to me Justin that you're showing what's available today because the, like the system went in and checked, let's say the websites today.
